{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = Shinto | image = Tagata jinja haiden 2020-12 ac (1).jpg | deity = Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami)
Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki | official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto = }} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . [[simple:Tagata Shrine]] ==Related pages== *[[Ōagata Shrine]] *[[Kanamara Matsuri]] *[[Phallic processions]] *[[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] ==References== {{reflist}} == External links == === English websites === *[http://www.tagatajinja.com/index.html 田縣神社トップページ│Tagata jinja New Site] (Japanese; official homepage of Tagata-Jinja) *[http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html Yamasa Institute Tagata Jinja - Hounen Matsuri site (English)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060414210835/http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html |date=2006-04-14 }} *[http://farstrider.net/Japan/Festivals/HounenMatsuri/ NameBright - Coming Soon] *[https://www.thoeny.org/tagata.html Phallus photos and bits of info] === Japanese websites === * {{Official website|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市{{coord|35|18|57|N|136|56|28|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tagata Shrine}} [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:Festivals in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:March observances]] [[Category:Sex festivals]] [[Category:Sexuality in Shinto]] [[Category:Komaki]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]]{{Atsuta Faith}}

B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki | official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto = }} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . [[simple:Tagata Shrine]] ==Related pages== *[[Ōagata Shrine]] *[[Kanamara Matsuri]] *[[Phallic processions]] *[[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] ==References== {{reflist}} == External links == === English websites === *[http://www.tagatajinja.com/index.html 田縣神社トップページ│Tagata jinja New Site] (Japanese; official homepage of Tagata-Jinja) *[http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html Yamasa Institute Tagata Jinja - Hounen Matsuri site (English)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060414210835/http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html |date=2006-04-14 }} *[http://farstrider.net/Japan/Festivals/HounenMatsuri/ NameBright - Coming Soon] *[https://www.thoeny.org/tagata.html Phallus photos and bits of info] === Japanese websites === * {{Official website|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市{{coord|35|18|57|N|136|56|28|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tagata Shrine}} [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:Festivals in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:March observances]] [[Category:Sex festivals]] [[Category:Sexuality in Shinto]] [[Category:Komaki]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:転送]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]]{{Atsuta Faith}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = Shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Tagata jinja haiden 2020-12 ac (1).jpg | deity = Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami)
Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki | official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto = }} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . Coming Soon] *[https://www.thoeny.org/tagata.html Phallus photos and bits of info] === Japanese websites === * {{Official website|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市{{coord|35|18|57|N|136|56|28|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tagata Shrine}} [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:Festivals in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:March observances]] [[Category:Sex festivals]] [[Category:Sexuality in Shinto]] [[Category:Komaki]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:転送]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]]{{Atsuta Faith}}

Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . {{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = Shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Tagata jinja haiden 2020-12 ac (1).jpg | deity = Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami)
Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki | official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto = }} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki | official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto = }} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . [[simple:Tagata Shrine]] ==Related pages== *[[Ōagata Shrine]] *[[Kanamara Matsuri]] *[[Phallic processions]] *[[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] ==References== {{reflist}} == External links == === English websites === *[http://www.tagatajinja.com/index.html 田縣神社トップページ│Tagata jinja New Site] (Japanese; official homepage of Tagata-Jinja) *[http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html Yamasa Institute Tagata Jinja - Hounen Matsuri site (English)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060414210835/http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html |date=2006-04-14 }} *[http://farstrider.net/Japan/Festivals/HounenMatsuri/ NameBright - Coming Soon] *[https://www.thoeny.org/tagata.html Phallus photos and bits of info] === Japanese websites === * {{Official website|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市{{coord|35|18|57|N|136|56|28|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tagata Shrine}} [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:Festivals in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:March observances]] [[Category:Sex festivals]] [[Category:Sexuality in Shinto]] [[Category:Komaki]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1 6]][[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Qq]][[ja:田縣神社]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]]{{Atsuta Faith}}

Coming Soon] *[https://www.thoeny.org/tagata.html Phallus photos and bits of info] === Japanese websites === * {{Official website|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市{{coord|35|18|57|N|136|56|28|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tagata Shrine}} [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:Festivals in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:March observances]] [[Category:Sex festivals]] [[Category:Sexuality in Shinto]] [[Category:Komaki]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[ja:田縣神社]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]]{{Atsuta Faith}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = Shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Tagata jinja haiden 2020-12 ac (1).jpg | deity = Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami)
Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki | official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto = }} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . [[simple:Tagata Shrine]] ==Related pages== *[[Ōagata Shrine]] *[[Kanamara Matsuri]] *[[Phallic processions]] *[[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] ==References== {{reflist}} == External links == === English websites === *[http://www.tagatajinja.com/index.html 田縣神社トップページ│Tagata jinja New Site] (Japanese; official homepage of Tagata-Jinja) *[http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html Yamasa Institute Tagata Jinja - Hounen Matsuri site (English)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060414210835/http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html |date=2006-04-14 }} *[http://farstrider.net/Japan/Festivals/HounenMatsuri/ NameBright - Coming Soon] *[https://www.thoeny.org/tagata.html Phallus photos and bits of info] === Japanese websites === * {{Official website|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市{{coord|35|18|57|N|136|56|28|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tagata Shrine}} [[Category:Coordinates on Wikidata]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Festivals in Japan]] [[Category:Festivals in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:March observances]] [[Category:Sex festivals]] [[Category:Sexuality in Shinto]] [[Category:Komaki]] [[Category:Owari clan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[ja:田縣神社]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]]{{Atsuta Faith}}

After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . {{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = Shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Tagata jinja haiden 2020-12 ac (1).jpg | deity = Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami)
Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki | official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto = }} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki | official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto = }} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . {{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = Shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Tagata jinja haiden 2020-12 ac (1).jpg | deity = Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami)
Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki | official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto = }} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . {{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = Shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Tagata jinja haiden 2020-12 ac (1).jpg | deity = Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami)
Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki | official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto = }} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line .
