revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = Shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Tagata jinja haiden 2020-12 ac (1).jpg | deity = Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami)
Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki | official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto = }} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . [[simple:Tagata Shrine]] ==Related pages== *[[Ōagata Shrine]] *[[Kanamara Matsuri]] *[[Phallic processions]] *[[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] ==References== {{reflist}} == External links == === English websites === *[http://www.tagatajinja.com/index.html 田縣神社トップページ│Tagata jinja New Site] (Japanese; official homepage of Tagata-Jinja) *[http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html Yamasa Institute Tagata Jinja - Hounen Matsuri site (English)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060414210835/http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html |date=2006-04-14 }} *[http://farstrider.net/Japan/Festivals/HounenMatsuri/ NameBright - Coming Soon] *[https://www.thoeny.org/tagata.html Phallus photos and bits of info] === Japanese websites === * {{Official website|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市{{coord|35|18|57|N|136|56|28|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} ]] ]] ]] {{Atsuta Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:goodbye $1 D]] [[Category:pages with inappropriate tagging as redirects]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Tagata Shrine]] [[ja:田縣神社]] [[sn:Tagata Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tagata Shrine}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = Shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Tagata jinja haiden 2020-12 ac (1).jpg | deity = Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami)
Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki|official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto =|12=draft}} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|12=draft}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . [[simple:Tagata Shrine]] ==Related pages== *[[Ōagata Shrine]] *[[Kanamara Matsuri]] *[[Phallic processions]] *[[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] ==References== {{reflist}} == External links == === English websites === *[http://www.tagatajinja.com/index.html 田縣神社トップページ│Tagata jinja New Site] (Japanese; official homepage of Tagata-Jinja) *[http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html Yamasa Institute Tagata Jinja - Hounen Matsuri site (English)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060414210835/http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html |date=2006-04-14 }} *[http://farstrider.net/Japan/Festivals/HounenMatsuri/ NameBright - Coming Soon] *[https://www.thoeny.org/tagata.html Phallus photos and bits of info] === Japanese websites === * {{Official website|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市{{coord|35|18|57|N|136|56|28|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} ]] ]] ]] {{Atsuta Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:goodbye $1 D]] [[Category:pages with inappropriate tagging as redirects]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Tagata Shrine]] [[ja:田縣神社]] [[sn:Tagata Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tagata Shrine}}

Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki|official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto =|12=draft|12=draft}} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|12=draft|12=draft}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . [[simple:Tagata Shrine]] ==Related pages== *[[Ōagata Shrine]] *[[Kanamara Matsuri]] *[[Phallic processions]] *[[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] ==References== {{reflist}} == External links == === English websites === *[http://www.tagatajinja.com/index.html 田縣神社トップページ│Tagata jinja New Site] (Japanese; official homepage of Tagata-Jinja) *[http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html Yamasa Institute Tagata Jinja - Hounen Matsuri site (English)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060414210835/http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html |date=2006-04-14 }} *[http://farstrider.net/Japan/Festivals/HounenMatsuri/ NameBright - Coming Soon] *[https://www.thoeny.org/tagata.html Phallus photos and bits of info] === Japanese websites === * {{Official website|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市{{coord|35|18|57|N|136|56|28|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} ]] {{Atsuta Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:goodbye $1 D]] [[Category:pages with inappropriate tagging as redirects]] }} [[en:Tagata Shrine]] [[ja:田縣神社]] [[sn:Tagata Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tagata Shrine}}

Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki|official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto =|12=draft|12=draft}} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|12=draft|12=draft}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . [[simple:Tagata Shrine]] ==Related pages== *[[Ōagata Shrine]] *[[Kanamara Matsuri]] *[[Phallic processions]] *[[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] ==References== {{reflist}} == External links == === English websites === *[http://www.tagatajinja.com/index.html 田縣神社トップページ│Tagata jinja New Site] (Japanese; official homepage of Tagata-Jinja) *[http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html Yamasa Institute Tagata Jinja - Hounen Matsuri site (English)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060414210835/http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html |date=2006-04-14 }} *[http://farstrider.net/Japan/Festivals/HounenMatsuri/ NameBright - Coming Soon] *[https://www.thoeny.org/tagata.html Phallus photos and bits of info] === Japanese websites === * {{Official website|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市{{coord|35|18|57|N|136|56|28|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} ]] {{Atsuta Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:goodbye $1 D]] [[Category:pages with inappropriate tagging as redirects]] }} [[en:Tagata Shrine]] [[ja:田縣神社]] [[sn:Tagata Shrine]] {{citation needed|Tagata Shrine}}

revision.comment: 135 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:田縣神社#豊年祭 up to rev 101910031
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=田縣神社 |画像=[[File:Tagata jinja haiden 2020-12 ac (1).jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|18|57.03|N|136|56|28.19|E}}}} |所在地=[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]田県町152 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|18|57.03|N|136|56|28.19|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[御歳神]]
玉姫命 |社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=[[流造]]銅板葺 |例祭=[[3月15日]]（豊年祭） |神事= }} '''田縣神社'''（たがたじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内小社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。 毎年3月に行なわれる[[豊年祭 (田縣神社)|豊年祭]]で知られる。「縣」（あがた、けん）は「県」の[[字体#旧字体|旧字体]]（[[字体#異体字|異体字]]とされることもある）であり、'''田県神社'''と表記されることもある。 == 祭神 == 祭神は[[御歳神]]（みとしのかみ）と玉姫命（たまひめのみこと）で、五穀豊穣と子孫繁栄の神である。社伝によれば、当地は[[大荒田命]]（おおあらたのみこと）の邸の一部で、邸内で五穀豊穣の神である御歳神を祀っていた。玉姫命は大荒田命の娘で、夫が亡くなった後に実家に帰り、母親として立派に子どもたちを育て上げるとともに父を助けて当地を開拓したので、その功を讃えて神として祀られるようになったという。 境内には[[男根]]をかたどった石などが多数祀られている。 == 歴史 == 創建の年代は不詳で、かなり古い神社である{{要出典|date=2017年9月}}。古い土着信仰に基づく神社で、子宝と農業の信仰を結びつけた神社でもある。[[延喜式神名帳]]にある「[[尾張国]]丹羽郡 田縣神社」、[[貞治]]3年（[[1364年]]）の『尾張国内神名牒』にある「従三位上 田方天神」に比定されている。現在地は旧[[春日井郡]]なので、後に遷座したことになる。 == 豊年祭 == {{main|豊年祭 (田縣神社)}} 毎年[[3月15日]]には[[豊年祭 (田縣神社)|豊年祭]]が行われる。「ほうねんさい」または「ほうねんまつり」と読み、「扁之古祭」（へのこ祭）とも呼ばれる。男達が大男茎形（おおおわせがた）と呼ばれる[[男根]]をかたどった[[神輿]]を担いで練り歩き、小ぶりな男根をかたどったものを[[巫女]]たちが抱えて練り歩く。それに触れると子を授かるという伝承がある。大男茎形は直径60センチ、長さ2メートル余りで、毎年新しく[[ヒノキ]]で作られ奉納されている。 この祭事は、男根を「天」、女陰を「地」と見立て、「天からの恵みにより、大地が潤い、五穀豊穣となる事と子宝に恵まれる」事を祈願するものである。春に行われる理由は、「新しい生命の誕生」をも意味するからである。 なお、少し離れた場所にある[[犬山市]]の[[大縣神社]]の豊年祭（別名「於祖々祭（おそそ祭）」）が対になっており、こちらは[[女陰]]をかたどった山車などが練り歩く。 === 特色 === おみやげ品として男根をかたどった[[飴]]や[[チョコレート]]、[[オブジェ]]などが売られている。 近年外国人に非常に人気が高く、外国人観光客も多く訪れる。 豊年祭の日に限り、神社近くに複数の[[駐車場]]が設けられる。 == ギャラリー == File:Tagata Shrine Festival 20090315 (田縣神社の豊年祭) - panoramio.jpg|鳥居 File:Tagata jinja okumiya 2020-12 ac (1).jpg|奥宮 File:Tagata Shrine 4.JPG|願掛け File:Ensured Phallus object, Tagata Shrine 2.jpg|珍宝窟 File:Ensured Phallus object, Tagata Shrine 1.jpg|神体 File:Phallus stone pole, Tagata Shrine.jpg|石柱 File:Phallus cake, Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine) 2.jpg|珍呆参 File:Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG|豊年祭 File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|豊年祭 == アクセス == * [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄小牧線|小牧線]] [[田県神社前駅]]から徒歩で約5分（約200m）。 == 関連書籍 == * 丹羽欽治（編）「奉献田縣神社復興関係丹羽文庫古文書及書画目録（昭和五十年二月十一日調）」1975年 * 中井文雄（著）「日本一の大麻羅づくり〜田県神社木匠・斎木国光翁聞書」亀山巌、1981年 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[大縣神社]] - [[愛知県]][[犬山市]]にある神社。田縣神社とは対照的に、女陰の形をした石が祀られている。 * [[金山神社 (川崎市)|金山神社]] - [[神奈川県]][[川崎市]]にある神社。かなまら祭で知られる。 * [[どんつく神社]] - 田縣神社と同種の巨大な木製の男性器を御神体とし、それを載せた神輿・山車を用いた[[どんつく祭]]が行われる。 * [[麻羅観音]] - [[山口県]][[長門市]]にある[[大内氏]]関連の寺。 == 外部リンク == * {{official|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市 {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかたしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:小牧市の建築物]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:神道と性]] [[Category:性的シンボル]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[田縣神社]] to [[Tagata Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=田縣神社 |画像=[[File:Tagata jinja haiden 2020-12 ac (1).jpg|250px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|18|57.03|N|136|56|28.19|E}}}} |所在地=[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]田県町152 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|18|57.03|N|136|56|28.19|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[御歳神]]
玉姫命 |社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=[[流造]]銅板葺 |例祭=[[3月15日]]（豊年祭） |神事= }} '''田縣神社'''（たがたじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内小社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。 毎年3月に行なわれる[[豊年祭 (田縣神社)|豊年祭]]で知られる。「縣」（あがた、けん）は「県」の[[字体#旧字体|旧字体]]（[[字体#異体字|異体字]]とされることもある）であり、'''田県神社'''と表記されることもある。 == 祭神 == 祭神は[[御歳神]]（みとしのかみ）と玉姫命（たまひめのみこと）で、五穀豊穣と子孫繁栄の神である。社伝によれば、当地は[[大荒田命]]（おおあらたのみこと）の邸の一部で、邸内で五穀豊穣の神である御歳神を祀っていた。玉姫命は大荒田命の娘で、夫が亡くなった後に実家に帰り、母親として立派に子どもたちを育て上げるとともに父を助けて当地を開拓したので、その功を讃えて神として祀られるようになったという。 境内には[[男根]]をかたどった石などが多数祀られている。 == 歴史 == 創建の年代は不詳で、かなり古い神社である{{要出典|date=2017年9月}}。古い土着信仰に基づく神社で、子宝と農業の信仰を結びつけた神社でもある。[[延喜式神名帳]]にある「[[尾張国]]丹羽郡 田縣神社」、[[貞治]]3年（[[1364年]]）の『尾張国内神名牒』にある「従三位上 田方天神」に比定されている。現在地は旧[[春日井郡]]なので、後に遷座したことになる。 == 豊年祭 == {{main|豊年祭 (田縣神社)}} 毎年[[3月15日]]には[[豊年祭 (田縣神社)|豊年祭]]が行われる。「ほうねんさい」または「ほうねんまつり」と読み、「扁之古祭」（へのこ祭）とも呼ばれる。男達が大男茎形（おおおわせがた）と呼ばれる[[男根]]をかたどった[[神輿]]を担いで練り歩き、小ぶりな男根をかたどったものを[[巫女]]たちが抱えて練り歩く。それに触れると子を授かるという伝承がある。大男茎形は直径60センチ、長さ2メートル余りで、毎年新しく[[ヒノキ]]で作られ奉納されている。 この祭事は、男根を「天」、女陰を「地」と見立て、「天からの恵みにより、大地が潤い、五穀豊穣となる事と子宝に恵まれる」事を祈願するものである。春に行われる理由は、「新しい生命の誕生」をも意味するからである。 なお、少し離れた場所にある[[犬山市]]の[[大縣神社]]の豊年祭（別名「於祖々祭（おそそ祭）」）が対になっており、こちらは[[女陰]]をかたどった山車などが練り歩く。 === 特色 === おみやげ品として男根をかたどった[[飴]]や[[チョコレート]]、[[オブジェ]]などが売られている。 近年外国人に非常に人気が高く、外国人観光客も多く訪れる。 豊年祭の日に限り、神社近くに複数の[[駐車場]]が設けられる。 == ギャラリー == File:Tagata Shrine Festival 20090315 (田縣神社の豊年祭) - panoramio.jpg|鳥居 File:Tagata jinja okumiya 2020-12 ac (1).jpg|奥宮 File:Tagata Shrine 4.JPG|願掛け File:Ensured Phallus object, Tagata Shrine 2.jpg|珍宝窟 File:Ensured Phallus object, Tagata Shrine 1.jpg|神体 File:Phallus stone pole, Tagata Shrine.jpg|石柱 File:Phallus cake, Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine) 2.jpg|珍呆参 File:Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG|豊年祭 File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|豊年祭 == アクセス == * [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄小牧線|小牧線]] [[田県神社前駅]]から徒歩で約5分（約200m）。 == 関連書籍 == * 丹羽欽治（編）「奉献田縣神社復興関係丹羽文庫古文書及書画目録（昭和五十年二月十一日調）」1975年 * 中井文雄（著）「日本一の大麻羅づくり〜田県神社木匠・斎木国光翁聞書」亀山巌、1981年 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat}} * [[大縣神社]] - [[愛知県]][[犬山市]]にある神社。田縣神社とは対照的に、女陰の形をした石が祀られている。 * [[金山神社 (川崎市)|金山神社]] - [[神奈川県]][[川崎市]]にある神社。かなまら祭で知られる。 * [[どんつく神社]] - 田縣神社と同種の巨大な木製の男性器を御神体とし、それを載せた神輿・山車を用いた[[どんつく祭]]が行われる。 * [[麻羅観音]] - [[山口県]][[長門市]]にある[[大内氏]]関連の寺。 == 外部リンク == * {{official|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市 {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかたしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:小牧市の建築物]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:神道と性]] [[Category:性的シンボル]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = Shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Tagata jinja haiden 2020-12 ac (1).jpg | deity = Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami)
Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki|official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto =|qq=draft|qq=draft}} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . [[simple:Tagata Shrine]] ==Related pages== *[[Ōagata Shrine]] *[[Kanamara Matsuri]] *[[Phallic processions]] *[[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] ==References== {{reflist}} == External links == === English websites === *[http://www.tagatajinja.com/index.html 田縣神社トップページ│Tagata jinja New Site] (Japanese; official homepage of Tagata-Jinja) *[http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html Yamasa Institute Tagata Jinja - Hounen Matsuri site (English)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060414210835/http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html |date=2006-04-14 }} *[http://farstrider.net/Japan/Festivals/HounenMatsuri/ NameBright - Coming Soon] *[https://www.thoeny.org/tagata.html Phallus photos and bits of info] === Japanese websites === * {{Official website|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市{{coord|35|18|57|N|136|56|28|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} ]] {{Atsuta Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:goodbye $1 D]] [[Category:pages with inappropriate tagging as redirects]] }} [[en:Tagata Shrine]] [[ja:田縣神社]] [[sn:Tagata Shrine]] {{citation needed|Tagata Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|田縣神社|version=101910031|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki|official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto =|qq=draft|qq=draft}} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . ==Related pages== *[[Ōagata Shrine]] *[[Kanamara Matsuri]] *[[Phallic processions]] *[[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] ==References== {{reflist}} == External links == === English websites === *[http://www.tagatajinja.com/index.html 田縣神社トップページ│Tagata jinja New Site] (Japanese; official homepage of Tagata-Jinja) *[http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html Yamasa Institute Tagata Jinja - Hounen Matsuri site (English)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060414210835/http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html |date=2006-04-14 }} *[http://farstrider.net/Japan/Festivals/HounenMatsuri/ NameBright - Coming Soon] *[https://www.thoeny.org/tagata.html Phallus photos and bits of info] === Japanese websites === * {{Official website|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市{{coord|35|18|57|N|136|56|28|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} ]] {{Atsuta Faith}} {{translated page|ja|田縣神社|version=101910031|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:goodbye $1 D]] [[Category:pages with inappropriate tagging as redirects]] }} [[simple:Tagata Shrine]] [[sn:Tagata Shrine]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]] [[ja:田縣神社]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = Shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Tagata jinja haiden 2020-12 ac (1).jpg | deity = Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami)
Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple}} in Komaki|official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto =|qq=draft|qq=draft}} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . ==Related pages== *[[Ōagata Shrine]] *[[Kanamara Matsuri]] *[[Phallic processions]] *[[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] ==References== {{reflist}} == External links == === English websites === *[http://www.tagatajinja.com/index.html 田縣神社トップページ│Tagata jinja New Site] (Japanese; official homepage of Tagata-Jinja) *[http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html Yamasa Institute Tagata Jinja - Hounen Matsuri site (English)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060414210835/http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html |date=2006-04-14 }} *[http://farstrider.net/Japan/Festivals/HounenMatsuri/ NameBright - Coming Soon] *[https://www.thoeny.org/tagata.html Phallus photos and bits of info] === Japanese websites === * {{Official website|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市{{coord|35|18|57|N|136|56|28|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} ]] {{Atsuta Faith}} [[Category:translated pages without meta categories]] {{translated page|ja|田縣神社|version=101910031|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:goodbye $1 D]] [[Category:pages with inappropriate tagging as redirects]] }} [[simple:Tagata Shrine]] [[sn:Tagata Shrine]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]] [[ja:田縣神社]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = Shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Tagata jinja haiden 2020-12 ac (1).jpg | deity = Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami)
Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyocho]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in Komaki|official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto =|qq=draft|qq=draft}} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . ==Related pages== *[[Ōagata Shrine]] *[[Kanamara Matsuri]] *[[Phallic processions]] *[[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] ==References== {{reflist}} == External links == === English websites === *[http://www.tagatajinja.com/index.html 田縣神社トップページ│Tagata jinja New Site] (Japanese; official homepage of Tagata-Jinja) *[http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html Yamasa Institute Tagata Jinja - Hounen Matsuri site (English)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060414210835/http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html |date=2006-04-14 }} *[http://farstrider.net/Japan/Festivals/HounenMatsuri/ NameBright - Coming Soon] *[https://www.thoeny.org/tagata.html Phallus photos and bits of info] === Japanese websites === * {{Official website|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市{{coord|35|18|57|N|136|56|28|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} ]] {{Atsuta Faith}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|田縣神社|version=101910031|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tagata Shrine]][[sn:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1 D]] [[Category:pages with inappropriate tagging as redirects]] }} [[simple:Tagata Shrine]] [[sn:Tagata Shrine]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]] [[ja:田縣神社]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = Shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Tagata jinja haiden 2020-12 ac (1).jpg | deity = Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami)
Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in Komaki|official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto =|qq=draft|qq=draft}} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . ==Related pages== *[[Ōagata Shrine]] *[[Kanamara Matsuri]] *[[Phallic processions]] *[[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] ==References== {{reflist}} == External links == === English websites === *[http://www.tagatajinja.com/index.html 田縣神社トップページ│Tagata jinja New Site] (Japanese; official homepage of Tagata-Jinja) *[http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html Yamasa Institute Tagata Jinja - Hounen Matsuri site (English)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060414210835/http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html |date=2006-04-14 }} *[http://farstrider.net/Japan/Festivals/HounenMatsuri/ NameBright - Coming Soon] *[https://www.thoeny.org/tagata.html Phallus photos and bits of info] === Japanese websites === * {{Official website|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市{{coord|35|18|57|N|136|56|28|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} ]] {{Atsuta Faith}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|田縣神社|version=101910031|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tagata Shrine]][[sn:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1 D]] [[Category:pages with inappropriate tagging as redirects]] }} [[simple:Tagata Shrine]] [[sn:Tagata Shrine]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]] [[ja:田縣神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Komaki]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | religious_affiliation = Shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Tagata jinja haiden 2020-12 ac (1).jpg | deity = Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami)
Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto) }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (田縣神社) is a small shrine located in [[Komaki]] City, [[Aichi Prefecture]], Japan, near [[Nagoya Airfield]].{{Cite book |last=Picken |first=Stuart D. B. |url=https://www.amazon.ca/Historical-Dictionary-Dictionaries-Religions-Philosophies-ebook/dp/B004MYFMR6/ref=sr_1_1?keywords=9780810873728&linkCode=qs&qid=1700275947&s=books&sr=1-1 |title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto |date=2010-12-28 |publisher=Scarecrow Press |edition=2 |language=English}}{{Rp|page=6}} Tagata Shrine is famous for its Harvest Festival [[Hōnensai]], or the penis festival held annually on March 15.{{Cite book |last=Knapp |first=Bettina L. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=5isFdtMGwVwC&dq=tagata+shrine&pg=PA246 |title=Women in Myth |date=1997-01-01 |publisher=SUNY Press |isbn=978-0-7914-3164-1 |language=en}} The festival has received large amounts of attention.https://www.e-periodica.ch/cntmng?pid=act-001%3A1959%3A16%3A%3A995 It is near [[Ōagata Shrine]] which has a similar yonic festival the Sunday before this festival.{{Cite web |last=D |first=John |date=2012-03-24 |title=Fertility festival (Oh-agata/ Oagata Jinja) |url=https://www.greenshinto.com/2012/03/24/hime-no-miya-festival-ogata-jinja/ |access-date=2023-04-27 |website=Green Shinto |language=en-GB}}{{Rp|page=6}} == History == The exact year of foundation for Tagata Shrine is unknown, but it is believed to be over 1500 years old. It is known for combining beliefs related to fertility and agriculture.{{Rp|page=6}} It is listed as a [[Myojin Taisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. == Harvest festival == {{Main|Hōnensai}} {{Infobox holiday | holiday_name = Hōnensai | type = shinto | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Hōnen Matsuri 2.JPG | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |imagesize = 250px | caption = Hōnensai at {{ill|Tagata Shrine|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagata Shrine|qq=}} in Komaki|official_name = | nickname = | observedby = Japan | litcolor = | longtype = Religious | significance = Celebrates the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility | begins = | ends = | scheduling = same day each year | date = 15 March | celebrations = | observances = | relatedto =|qq=draft|qq=draft}} [[File:Hōnen Matsuri 6.JPG|thumb|Hōnensai]] {{Nihongo|Harvest Festival|豊年祭|Hōnensai}} is a [[fertility]] [[festival]] celebrated every year on March 15 at the shrine. ''Hōnen'' means ''prosperous year'' in Japanese, implying a rich harvest, while a ''[[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' is a festival. The Hōnen festival and ceremony celebrate the blessings of a bountiful harvest and all manner of prosperity and fertility. [[File:Tagata Jinja (entrance).png|thumb|Tagata Jinja (田縣神社) in Komaki, Tagata, [[Aichi Prefecture]] ]] The venerated Shinto deities are Mitoshi (御歳神, Mitoshi-no-kami) and female deity Tamahime (玉姫命, Tamahime-no-mikoto). Mitoshi is the son of the Shinto male deity [[Toshigami]] (年神) or known by local as Ōtoshi (大歳神, Ōtoshi-no-kami) and grandson of the Shinto deity [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]. Tamahime is a princess and the daughter of Ō'arata (大荒田命, Ō'arata-no-Mikoto), the matriarch of [[Owari clan|''Owari'' clan]] (尾張氏) of her husband {{ill|Takeinadane|ja|建稲種命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane|qq=}} (健稲種命, Take'inadane-no-mikoto) who were blessed with two sons and four daughters. After her husband's death, she returned to his hometown Arata (situated close to Komaki), encouraged to cultivate with the help of her father Ō'arata, honor and achieved his achievements.{{Cite web |title=Small village gets real big at ancient celebration |url=https://www.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil/Iwakuni-News/News-Stories/News-Article-Display/Article/506486/small-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration/https%3A%2F%2Fwww.mcasiwakuni.marines.mil%2FIwakuni-News%2FNews-Stories%2FNews-Article-Display%2FArticle%2F506486%2Fsmall-village-gets-real-big-at-ancient-celebration%2F |access-date=2023-11-18 |website=Marine Corps Air Station Iwakuni, Japan |language=en-US}} [[File:Youbutsu (O-owasegata).png|thumb|the shrine of the ''Youbutsu'' (陽物, lit. "the male object") or ''ō-owase-gata'' (大男茎形, lit. "the grand Phallus shape/ object") ]] In [[Inuyama, Aichi|Inuyama City]], there is another festival Sunday before at [[Ōagata Shrine]] (大縣神社). This festival includes floats shaped like a [[vulva]], which complement the phallic-shaped mikoshi used in the festival. {{commons Category|Hōnen Matsuri (Tagata Shrine)}} == Gallery == File:Tagata_Shrine_Festival_20090315_(田縣神社の豊年祭)_-_panoramio.jpg|alt=鳥居|torii File:Tagata_jinja_okumiya_2020-12_ac_(1).jpg|alt=奥宮|Okumiya File:Tagata_Shrine_4.JPG|alt=願掛け|wish File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_2.jpg|alt=珍宝窟|treasure cave File:Ensured_Phallus_object,_Tagata_Shrine_1.jpg|alt=神体|object of worship File:Phallus_stone_pole,_Tagata_Shrine.jpg|alt=石柱|stone pillar File:Phallus_cake,_Hōnen_Matsuri_(Tagata_Shrine)_2.jpg|alt=珍呆参|pastry File:Hōnen_Matsuri_2.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival File:Hōnen_Matsuri_6.JPG|alt=豊年祭|harvest festival == Access == * About 5 minutes on foot (about 200m) from [[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] on the Meitetsu Komaki Line . ==Related pages== *[[Ōagata Shrine]] *[[Kanamara Matsuri]] *[[Phallic processions]] *[[Tagata-jinja-mae Station]] ==References== {{reflist}} == External links == === English websites === *[http://www.tagatajinja.com/index.html 田縣神社トップページ│Tagata jinja New Site] (Japanese; official homepage of Tagata-Jinja) *[http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html Yamasa Institute Tagata Jinja - Hounen Matsuri site (English)] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060414210835/http://www.yamasa.org/japan/english/destinations/aichi/tagata_jinja.html |date=2006-04-14 }} *[http://farstrider.net/Japan/Festivals/HounenMatsuri/ NameBright - Coming Soon] *[https://www.thoeny.org/tagata.html Phallus photos and bits of info] === Japanese websites === * {{Official website|http://www.tagatajinja.com/}} * [http://www.city.komaki.aichi.jp/admin/event_1/midokoro/kankou/2/10628.html 田縣神社の豊年祭] 小牧市{{coord|35|18|57|N|136|56|28|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} ]] {{Atsuta Faith}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|田縣神社|version=101910031|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tagata Shrine]][[sn:Tagata Shrine]][[Category:goodbye $1 D]] [[Category:pages with inappropriate tagging as redirects]] }} [[simple:Tagata Shrine]] [[sn:Tagata Shrine]] [[en:Tagata Shrine]] [[ja:田縣神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Komaki]] [[Category:小牧市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Human gender and sexuality symbols]]
