Tamahime-no-Mikoto |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded=Unknown |Main hall={{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper-sheet roofing |Annual festival={{ill|lt=March 15|Q2403|WD=Q2403|1=March 15}} (Hōnen Festival) |Rituals= |Map= {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Tagata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tagata Shrine''' (たがたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Komaki City|Q389537|WD=Q389537|1=Komaki}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=minor Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is known for the {{ill|lt=Hōnen Festival (Tagata Shrine)|Q30924149|WD=Q30924149|lt=Hōnen Festival|1=Hōnensai}} held every March. The character “縣” (agata, ken) is the {{ill|lt=old character form|Q137673972|WD=Q137673972|lt=old form|1=Chinese character forms}} of “県” (and is sometimes considered a {{ill|lt=variant character|Q137673972|WD=Q137673972|lt=variant|1=Chinese character forms}}), so it is also written as '''Tagata Shrine''' (田県神社). === Enshrined deities === The enshrined deities are {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mitoshi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} (みとしのかみ) and Tamahime-no-Mikoto (たまひめのみこと), gods of abundant harvests and the prosperity of descendants. According to shrine tradition, this area was part of the estate of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Oarata-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, where Mitoshi-no-Kami, a god of bountiful crops, was worshiped. Tamahime-no-Mikoto was the daughter of Oarata-no-Mikoto; after her husband’s death, she returned to her parental home, raised her children admirably as a mother, and helped her father cultivate the land. In praise of these merits, she came to be enshrined as a deity. Within the shrine grounds, many stones shaped like {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=phallic symbols|1=UNKNOWN}} are enshrined. === History === The date of foundation is unknown, and it is considered a very old shrine {{citation needed|date=September 2017}}. It is a shrine based on ancient indigenous beliefs and is associated with phallic worship believed to improve {{ill|lt=male genital|Q842083|WD=Q842083|1=Male reproductive system}} fortune, linking faith in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=fertility|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|lt=agriculture|Q11451|WD=Q11451|1=Agriculture}}. It is identified with “Tagata Shrine of Niwa District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}” listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and with “Tagata Tenjin, Senior Third Rank” recorded in the *Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō* from the third year of {{ill|lt=Jōji|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} ({{ill|lt=1364|Q6185|WD=Q6185|1=1364}}). Since the current location was formerly in {{ill|lt=Kasugai District|Q11513576|WD=Q11513576|1=Kasugai district}}, the shrine must have been relocated at a later time. === Hōnen Festival === {{main|Hōnen Festival (Tagata Shrine)}} Every year on {{ill|lt=March 15|Q2403|WD=Q2403|1=March 15}}, the {{ill|lt=Hōnen Festival (Tagata Shrine)|Q30924149|WD=Q30924149|lt=Hōnen Festival|1=Hōnensai}} is held. It is designated as an Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Komaki City and is read as “Hōnensai” or “Hōnenmatsuri,” also known as the “Henoko Festival.” Men parade while carrying a {{ill|lt=mikoshi|Q979940|WD=Q979940|1=Mikoshi}} bearing a large phallus called Ōowase-gata, while {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|WD=Q1047139|1=Miko}} carry smaller phallic objects. There is a tradition that touching them grants fertility. The Ōowase-gata measures about 60 cm in diameter and over 2 meters in length, and a new one is made each year from {{ill|lt=Japanese cypress|Q917187|WD=Q917187|1=Chamaecyparis obtusa}} and dedicated to the shrine. This ritual symbolizes the phallus as “heaven” and the vulva as “earth,” praying that “through blessings from heaven, the land is nourished, resulting in abundant harvests and fertility.” The reason it is held in spring is that it also signifies “the birth of new life.” Nearby, the Hōnen Festival of {{ill|lt=Ōagata Shrine|Q135732|WD=Q135732|1=Ōagata Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Inuyama City|Q503411|WD=Q503411|1=Inuyama, Aichi}}—also known as the “Ososo Festival”—forms a pair with this festival; there, floats shaped like {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=vulva|1=UNKNOWN}} are paraded. === Characteristics === As souvenirs, candies, chocolates, and {{ill|lt=objects|Q7075109|WD=Q7075109|1=Objet d'art}} shaped like phallic symbols are sold. In recent years, it has become extremely popular with foreigners, and many foreign tourists visit.
